content.problemStatement:
Ada is training for her match against Magnus Carlsen in the World Chess Championship 2020.

Ada took a standard chessboard with 888 rows (numbered 111 through 888) and 888 columns (numbered 111 through 888); let's denote a cell in row rrr and column ccc by (r,c)(r, c)(r,c). Then, Ada placed a bishop in a cell (r0,c0)(r_0, c_0)(r0​,c0​); it is guaranteed that this cell is black. Ada's goal is to move the bishop in such a way that it visits all black cells.

Remember that a bishop is a piece that moves diagonally ― formally, the bishop may move from a cell (rs,cs)(r_s, c_s)(rs​,cs​) to a cell (rt,ct)(r_t, c_t)(rt​,ct​) if and only if either rs+cs=rt+ctr_s+c_s=r_t+c_trs​+cs​=rt​+ct​ or rs−cs=rt−ctr_s-c_s=r_t-c_trs​−cs​=rt​−ct​. In such a move, the bishop visits all cells between (rs,cs)(r_s, c_s)(rs​,cs​) and (rt,ct)(r_t, c_t)(rt​,ct​) on this diagonal (inclusive).

Help Ada find a sequence of at most 646464 moves for the bishop such that when the bishop follows this route, it visits all black cells on the chessboard. Note that each cell may be visited multiple times and it is not necessary to return to the starting point.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T. Each test case contains two space-separated integers r0 and c0.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a line containing a single integer MMM (M≤64) ― the number of moves in the bishop's route. Then, print MMM lines. For each iii (1≤i≤M), the iii-th of these lines should contain two space-separated integers rir_i and cic_i, where the route followed by the bishop is (r0,c0)→(r1,c1)→(r2,c2)→…→(rM,cM).

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by simulating the movement of the bishop on the chessboard. We can use a queue to store the moves of the bishop. In each move, we can calculate the new position of the bishop based on the current position and the direction of the move. We can use a set to keep track of the visited cells to avoid visiting the same cell multiple times.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * M) where T is the number of test cases and M is the maximum number of moves in a test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(T * M) where T is the number of test cases and M is the maximum number of moves in a test case.

code.Python3:
import sys

T = int(input())

for _ in range(T):
    r0, c0 = map(int, input().split())
    M = 0
    r = r0
    c = c0
    dir = 1

    while True:
        print(f'{r} {c}')
        M += 1
        if r < 1 or r > 888 or c < 1 or c > 888:
            break
        if r + c == r0 + c0:
            break
        if r + c < r0 + c0:
            r += 1
            c -= 1
        else:
            r -= 1
            c += 1
    print(M)
